step_by_step_thinking:
The given data includes the following details:
- Total births = 456
- Stillbirths (no signs of life at birth) = 56
- Deaths < 28 days old = 56
- Deaths > 28 days but ≤ 1 year = 34
- Total deaths in the year = 500
To calculate the infant mortality rate, we use the formula:
\[ \text{Infant Mortality Rate} = \left( \frac{\text{Number of infant deaths under 1 year}}{\text{Total number of live births}} \right) \times 1000 \]
From the information above, the number of infant deaths under 1 year is \(56 + 34 = 90\).
Thus,
\[ \text{Infant Mortality Rate} = \left( \frac{90}{456} \right) \times 1000 \approx 196.72 \% \]
This rounds approximately to option **A**.

- Deaths < 28 days old = 56
- Deaths > 28 days but ≤ 1 year = 34
- Total deaths in the year = 500
To calculate the infant mortality rate, we use the formula:
\[ \text{Infant Mortality Rate} = \left( \frac{\text{Number of infant deaths under 1 year}}{\text{Total number of live births}} \right) \times 1000 \]
From the information above, the number of infant deaths under 1 year is \(56 + 34 = 90\).
